Class GroceryManagement

java.lang.Object
GroceryManagement
public class GroceryManagement extends Object
GroceryManagement handles inventory operations such as adding, removing, and searching grocery items.

  • Constructor Details

    • GroceryManagement

      public GroceryManagement()
      Default Constructor

  • Method Details

    • displayUI

      public static void displayUI()
      Function: displayUI() Prerequisites: None. Description: Prints a simple menu to the console showing the available inventory system options: 1) Add Inventory 2) View Inventory 3) Restock Inventory 4) Exit Post: The menu text is displayed to the console. No variables or arrays are modified.

    • addInventory

      public static void addInventory(Scanner sc, String[] names, double[] prices, int[] stocks)
      Searches for the first empty slot in names (where names[i] == null). Prompts the user for item name, price, and stock quantity. Stores values at the same index in all three arrays.
      Parameters:
      sc - Scanner used for user input
      names - array of item names
      prices - array of item prices
      stocks - array of stock quantities

    • printInventory

      public static void printInventory(String[] names, double[] prices, int[] stocks)
      Prints the current inventory items to the console.

      For each index i, the method prints the item name, price, and stock if names[i] != null. Empty slots are skipped.

      Parameters:
      names - array of item names
      prices - array of item prices
      stocks - array of item stock quantities Implementation Note: This method does not modify any arrays.

    • restockItem

      public static void restockItem(String[] names, int[] stocks, String target, int amount)
      Restocks an existing inventory item by adding a given amount to its stock.

      The method searches for target inside names. If found at index i, it increases stocks[i] by amount.

      Parameters:
      names - array of item names
      stocks - array of item stock quantities (same length as names)
      target - the name of the item to restock
      amount - the amount of stock to add Implementation Note: If the target item is not found, the method prints "Item not found".

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      Program entry point.

      Creates inventory arrays, displays the menu, and processes user input in a loop until the user chooses to exit.

      Parameters:
      args - command-line arguments (not used)
